title: Maba lanceolata
intro: Maba lanceolata, also known as the Chinese apple or Chinese plum, is a plant species belonging to the family Sapotaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antioxidant properties, which are said to aid in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. The bioactive constituents of Maba lanceolata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in traditional Chinese medicine to make patent medicines and other preparations.
taxonomy: Maba lanceolata is classified within the family Sapotaceae. This classification places it in the order Ebenales, which is a group of flowering plants. The family Sapotaceae is a part of the larger clade of asterids within the angiosperm clade. The order Ebenales is a subset of the clade of flowering plants known as the core eudicots. The classification of Maba lanceolata also places it within the subclass Dilleniidae.
uses: Maba lanceolata is known to have several medicinal uses. The plant's extracts have been studied for their potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. In traditional medicine, Maba lanceolata is used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems. The plant is also believed to have antiviral and antibacterial properties, which may help in combating infections. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Maba lanceolata for medicinal purposes.

The roots are known to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. In traditional medicine, the roots are used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. side_effects: Maba lanceolata is a plant with some potential side effects, although more research is needed for a comprehensive understanding.
